input: Analyze a 10x Visium spatial transcriptomics dataset from a melanoma biopsy. Identify spatially variable genes and characterize the tumor microenvironment.
Perform spot-level cell-type deconvolution on Visium data using a matched scRNA-seq reference. Report cell-type proportions.
Identify spatially co-localized ligand-receptor pairs between tumor cells and immune cells in a spatial transcriptomics dataset.
Identify distinct spatial domains (tissue regions) in a brain tissue Visium dataset without pre-defined annotations.

expected_details.expected_domains: should correspond to known brain regions (cortex layers, white matter, hippocampus)

input: Analyze MERFISH data from a breast cancer sample. 500 genes measured at subcellular resolution. Characterize cell types and neighborhoods.

expected_details.subcellular_advantage: can resolve RNA localization within cells (nuclear vs cytoplasmic)

input: Identify spatially variable genes during mouse embryo development (E14.5) from a spatial transcriptomics dataset.
Integrate spatial transcriptomics data from 3 tumor samples (responder vs non-responder to immunotherapy). Identify spatially differential features.

expected_details.key_comparison: immune infiltration patterns in responders vs non-responders

input: Identify cellular niches (co-occurring cell-type combinations) in a colorectal cancer Visium dataset.

expected_details.clinical_relevance: immune niche composition predicts ICI response

input: Compare analysis approaches for Slide-seq (10 μm resolution) vs Visium (55 μm spots). How does resolution affect deconvolution?

expected_details.slideseq: near single-cell — direct cell typing possible but noisier
comparison: Slide-seq: less need for deconvolution but lower sensitivity per bead
Visium: better sensitivity but spot = mixture

input: Map spatial transcriptomic gradients along the crypt-villus axis in intestinal tissue.

approach: define spatial axis from crypt base to villus tip
identify genes with gradient expression along axis
expected_gradients: stem cell markers (LGR5) at crypt base
differentiation markers (ALPI, SI) at villus tip
